settings.title: A Marquee Wedding on the Family Farm
settings.summary: We built a full wedding from scratch in a field with no power, water, or kitchen.
settings.description:
Eleanor and Tom married in a field on her parents' farm, which meant starting with bare grass and a lovely view and nothing else. A farm field is a blank site: no power, no running water, no kitchen, no loos, and no fixed parking. Every one of those had to be brought in and made to work for a single day.

The infrastructure

We treated the field like a temporary venue and built it up piece by piece. The marquee set the footprint, and around it we coordinated the services that a building would normally provide:

  • Generators sized for the marquee lighting, band, and caterers
  • A water bowser to supply the kitchens and washrooms
  • Portable kitchens so the caterers could cook on site
  • Accessible loos for all 130 guests

Movement and access

Beyond the services, we laid out a clear plan for parking on the field and ran an evening shuttle into the village so guests who were drinking did not have to drive. Getting people in and out smoothly matters as much as the marquee itself, and on a working farm the routes had to suit the ground as well as the cars.

Pulling it together

On the day the infrastructure simply worked in the background, which is exactly what you want from it. So 130 guests saw a marquee in a beautiful field and none of the logistics holding it up, and the family got to enjoy the wedding rather than run it.
